criteria.description: Code is clean, well-structured, follows best practices. Proper error handling, no code smells, good naming conventions.
Code works correctly with minor style or structure issues. Follows most conventions.
Code works but has noticeable issues: poor naming, inconsistent patterns, missing edge cases.
Code has significant quality issues, hard to read, minimal error handling.
Code barely works or doesn't work. No structure, no error handling, security concerns.
Optimal solution with consideration of edge cases, performance, and scalability.
Good solution that solves the main problem. Misses some edge cases.
Solution works for the happy path but has logical gaps or inefficiencies.
Solution is flawed or incomplete. Major logical gaps.
Solution doesn't address the problem or introduces new issues.
Clear code comments, meaningful variable names, excellent documentation of approach.
Mostly clear with minor documentation gaps.
Some documentation but unclear in places. Hard to follow the reasoning.
Minimal documentation. Code is hard to follow.
No documentation, misleading comments, very hard to understand.
All requirements met. Handles all states: loading, empty, error, success, edge cases.
Most requirements met. Minor edge cases not handled.
Core requirements met but several missing pieces.
Significant requirements not addressed.
Most requirements not met. Incomplete submission.
